q: Aeroplane : Pilot :: Car : ?
e: Pilot flies an aeroplane; driver drives a car.

q: Wing : Bird :: Fin : ?
e: A wing helps a bird fly; a fin helps a fish swim.

q: Sky : Cloud :: Sea : ?
e: Clouds move in the sky; waves move in the sea.

q: Airport : Aeroplane :: Harbour : ?
e: Aeroplanes dock at airports; ships dock at harbours.

q: Radar : Detection :: Compass : ?
Doctor : Patient :: Pilot : ?
e: Doctor cares for patient; pilot carries passenger.

q: Helmet : Head :: Seatbelt : ?
e: A helmet protects head; a seatbelt secures waist/lap.

q: Engine : Fuel :: Body : ?
e: Engine runs on fuel; body runs on food.

q: Wheel : Move :: Wing : ?
e: Wheel enables motion; wing generates lift.

q: Storm : Calm :: War : ?
A, C, F, J, O, ?
Z, X, V, T, R, ?
Odd one out: Aeroplane, Helicopter, Rocket, Bicycle.
Odd one out: Pilot, Captain, Driver, Engineer.
Odd one out: Boeing, Airbus, Lockheed, Toyota.
e: Toyota makes cars; others make aircraft.

q: Odd one out: Altitude, Velocity, Acceleration, Humidity.
e: Humidity is not a kinematic quantity.

q: Odd one out: 4, 9, 25, 27.
Odd one out: Karachi, Islamabad, Quetta, Dubai.
Odd one out: JF-17, F-16, Mirage, Agosta.
e: Agosta is a submarine; others are aircraft.

q: Odd one out: Doctor, Engineer, Pilot, Plumber.
e: Plumber is a trade, not a profession requiring formal degree.

q: Odd one out: Hydrogen, Helium, Oxygen, Brass.
Odd one out: Radar, Sonar, Lidar, Telephone.
e: Telephone is communication; others are ranging/detection.

q: If PLANE is coded as QMBOF, FLY = ?
e: Each letter +1: F→G, L→M, Y→Z → GMZ.

q: If FLY is coded as 6-12-25, JET = ?
If 'PILOT' is coded as 'QJMPU', 'CREW' = ?
e: Each letter +1: C→D, R→S, E→F, W→X → DSFX.

q: If A=1, FLY = ?
If CLOUD = 15, RAIN = ?
e: Letter position sums mod or specific pattern.

q: If 'WING' is coded as 'XJOH', 'TAIL' = ?
e: Each letter +1: T→U, A→B, I→J, L→M → UBJM.

q: If JET = 10-5-20, what does 7-5-20 spell?
If SKY = 19-11-25, FLY = ?
If 'AIRCRAFT' is coded as 'BJSDSBGU', 'JET' = ?
A man said, 'His father is my father's only son.' Referring to his:
e: Father's only son = himself; the boy's father = him → his son.

q: Pointing to a girl, a man said, 'She is the daughter of my wife's daughter.' The girl is his:
e: His wife's daughter = his daughter; her daughter = his granddaughter.

q: A woman says, 'He is the son of my grandfather's only child.' The man is her:
e: Grandfather's only child = her parent → parent's son = her brother.

q: A is B's brother. C is B's son. D is C's sister. How is A related to D?
e: D is B's daughter; A is B's brother → A is D's uncle.

q: If a man's wife is the sister of my sister, how is the man related to me?
e: My sister's sister = me (if female) or my sister; her husband is my brother-in-law.

q: A points to a photograph and says, 'She is my mother's only daughter's daughter.' She is A's:
e: Mother's only daughter = A (if female); her daughter = A's daughter.

q: If Ahmed is the brother of Bilal's sister, who is Ahmed to Bilal?
Ali points to a boy: 'His mother is the only daughter of my mother.' The boy is Ali's:
e: Only daughter of Ali's mother = Ali (if female); but Ali is male → so his sister. Boy's mother = Ali's sister → boy is Ali's nephew.

q: A man's father has only one child. The man's sister has a daughter. The man is the girl's:
e: If father had only one child (the man), the 'sister' must be his wife's sister — but more commonly the riddle implies maternal uncle.

q: X introduces Y as 'the son of my father's son'. Y is X's:
e: Father's son = X (or brother); Y's father = X → Y is X's son.

q: A plane flies 300 km north, then 400 km east. Distance from start?
A pilot facing north turns 90° right, then 90° right. Now facing?
A plane takes off heading east, makes a 270° clockwise turn. Now heading?
A ship sails 8 km east then 6 km north. Bearing from start?
A man walks 5 km west, 5 km south, 5 km east, 5 km north. Where is he?
If you face north and turn 135° clockwise, you face:
A plane flying west turns left 90°. Now heading?
At 6 pm, the sun sets roughly in which direction?
A drone moves 3 km north, 4 km west. Distance from origin?
A pilot facing east turns 180°. Now facing?
Which does not belong: Wing, Tail, Engine, Wheel?
e: Wheels are not standard flight parts.

q: Which does not belong: Take-off, Landing, Hover, Swim?
Which does not belong: Boeing, Airbus, Lockheed, Ferrari?
Which does not belong: Pencil, Eraser, Ruler, Cockpit?
Which does not belong: 7, 11, 13, 15?
Which does not belong: Oxygen, Hydrogen, Helium, Water?
Which does not belong: Altitude, Velocity, Wing, Thrust?
e: Wing is a part; others are quantities.

q: Which does not belong: Cyclone, Hurricane, Typhoon, Earthquake?
Which does not belong: Newton, Pascal, Watt, Darwin?
Which does not belong: Propeller, Jet, Rocket, Wheel?
e: Wheel doesn't provide thrust for flight.

q: If 5 planes carry 5×5 = 25 passengers per plane, total passengers in 5 planes?
e: 25 per plane × 5 = 125; but the common riddle answer is 25.

q: A plane climbs at 1000 ft/min. Time to reach 35000 ft?
If 2 mechanics fix 2 planes in 2 hours, how long for 4 mechanics to fix 4 planes?
e: Each mechanic fixes 1 plane in 2 hrs.

q: A train 150 m long passes a pole in 6 seconds. Speed?

If today is Tuesday, what day will it be 50 days from now?
A man is 4 times as old as his son. In 20 years he will be twice as old. Son's age now?
Engine : Power :: Battery : ?
e: Engine produces power; battery produces energy.

q: Cockpit : Pilot :: Bridge : ?
e: Cockpit is pilot's station; bridge is captain's.

q: Bird : Wing :: Aircraft : ?
Stall : Fall :: Brakes : ?
e: Stall can cause fall; brake failure can cause skid.

q: Propeller : Thrust :: Wing : ?
e: Propeller generates thrust; wing generates lift.

q: Take-off : Departure :: Landing : ?
e: Take-off starts departure; landing ends with arrival.

q: Pilot : Goggles :: Soldier : ?
Cloud : Rain :: Volcano : ?
e: Cloud emits rain; volcano emits lava.

q: Stratosphere : Ozone :: Troposphere : ?
Wheel : Rotate :: Hinge : ?
e: A wheel rotates; a hinge swings.
